summary: Upgraded the selected Path Map chapter's Chapter Command Log into a generated-art Path Chapter Command Relay console for local bot handoff visibility.

queries: gamified dashboard agent team progress UI examples game-like analytics dashboard
game mission control dashboard UI level progression achievements design
minigame hub UI progression map dashboard design
pattern_notes: Command surfaces should clarify the next action and local state before asking an operator to queue anything.
Mission-control references work best when they show queue pressure, history, and context in one glanceable panel.
The relay must remain local-only and data-derived so future agent-company forks inherit it without bot-specific hardcoding.

generated_asset.prompt_summary: Premium dark sci-fi chapter-to-bot command relay console with holographic signal spine, queue nodes, teal/amber/green glows, no text, no logos, and no characters.

ui_contract: Render a path-chapter-command-log panel with generated Path Chapter Command Relay art.
Derive relay stats from staged dispatches, browser-local dispatch history, lane suggestion urgency, chapter item count, and gated command records.
Expose Q, COM, and TRL controls through existing local-only staging and detail-view handlers.
Register the relay texture in the Visual Asset Vault system assets.
Keep scan animation transform/opacity-based and covered by reduced-motion handling.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_command_log.py -q failed before implementation on missing pathChapterCommandRelayStats.
red_green.green: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_command_log.py -q passed after generated asset, component, styles, docs, cache token, and Asset Vault wiring.

responsive.evidence: All checked viewports rendered the Path Chapter Command Relay with generated backdrop, signal visual, six stats, four command entries, three local actions, zero bad rects, zero button text overflow, zero page horizontal overflow, and zero console errors.
